fullArticle:
As can be seen by Taylor Sheridan's Yellowstone universe, the Paramount powerhouse that dominates the streaming charts with the likes of The Madison and Dutton Ranch, the Western renaissance on the small screen continues. Recently, in many countries (although sadly not the U.S.) Yellowstone was picked up by Netflix, who are never one to miss out on a trend. They also released Ransom Canyon, a Texas Hill Country-set series created by April Blair, that earned a second season renewal set for later this month.

But the streamer's biggest bet on a Western project in quite some time has come in the form of an adaptation of a beloved series of Western novels, which also became a hugely popular TV series that ran between 1974 and 1983. Of course, we're talking about Little House on the Prairie, Ne...
